title: Rethinking the C-Suite & Bob Sternfels on McKinsey's Future
content:
In fast-moving industries and volatile markets, clear strategy is supposed to come directly from the C-level. However, when executives fail to provide direction, their teams quickly lose steam and become unsure of their actual destination. When strategic decisions stall, senior leaders must immediately step up to translate ambiguity into clarity and maintain forward momentum. Experts estimate that this strategic indecision costs Fortune 500 companies hundreds of millions of dollars in lost productivity every year. One of the most effective ways to overcome this hesitation is to use real data to demonstrate how delays damage employee morale.

Bob Sternfels, McKinsey & Company’s global managing partner, has had to navigate similar leadership challenges while restoring his firm's reputation. Since taking over in 2021, he has tightened client selection rules without alienating the firm’s powerful partners. To successfully set McKinsey up for the artificial intelligence era, he is driving a massive organizational transformation. This new strategy focuses less on traditional consulting services and much more on delivering tangible, long-term outcomes. Sternfels recognizes that the half-life of companies is rapidly shortening as the rate of technological change continues to quicken.

As McKinsey approaches its centennial, Sternfels emphasizes the crucial importance of effectively managing organizational change alongside new technologies. When companies implement AI, they must completely redesign their workflows to ensure maximum speed and efficiency. A flatter organization that cuts out middle management can significantly accelerate a company's ability to adapt. However, Sternfels notes that successfully aligning executives on these massive structural changes often takes much longer than anticipated. Ultimately, mastering this internal adaptation will determine which firms survive and thrive in the coming decades.
